_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Intense facial pain affecting day to day activity & can be triggered by common activities such as eating, talking, shaving, brushing, chewing
Recurrent paroxysms of unilateral facial pain in the distribution(s) of one or more divisions of the trigeminal nerve, with no radiation beyond, and fulfilling criteria B and C (ICHD-3 criteria)
Pain lasting from a fraction of a second to two minutes
Pain is electric shock-like, shooting, stabbing or sharp in quality
Pain precipitated by innocuous stimuli within the affected trigeminal distribution
Not better accounted for by another ICHD-3 diagnosis

preauth.label: Clinical notes including clinical evaluation, indication of procedure, and planned line of management

ppd_preauth.q: Are clinical notes detailing history, signs & symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of treatment available?
Did the patient present with intense facial pain affecting day to day activity & can be triggered by common activities such as eating, talking, shaving, brushing, chewing?
Did MRI Brain/MRI Angio imaging show the status of artery in C.P angle?
Was clinical presentation and imaging indicative of surgery?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the history, clinical evaluation, and imaging indicative of surgery?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge available?

common_queries: Clinical presentation or imaging was not indicative of surgery.
Clinical notes detailing history, signs & symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of treatment are missing or incomplete.
MRI Brain/MRI Angio imaging showing the status of artery in C.P angle is missing or does not support the indication.
Patient did not present with intense facial pain affecting day to day activity or pain that can be triggered by common activities.
Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with daily vitals and treatment details are missing or incomplete.
Detailed procedure / operative notes are missing or incomplete.
Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge is missing or incomplete.

clinical_key_pointers: Trigeminal neuralgia (TN) (also known as tic douloureux) is characterized by recurrent brief episodes of unilateral electric shock-like pains, abrupt in onset and termination, in the distribution of one or more divisions of the fifth cranial (trigeminal) nerve that typically are triggered by innocuous stimuli.
surgical_options: Microvascular decompression – a major neurosurgical procedure that involves craniotomy and the removal or separation of various vascular structures, often an ectatic superior cerebellar artery, away from the trigeminal nerve

Rhizotomy – encompasses a number of percutaneous surgical techniques that are performed by passing a cannula through the foramen ovale under fluoroscopic guidance, followed by lesioning of the trigeminal ganglion or root using one of several options: Radiofrequency thermocoagulation rhizotomy, Mechanical balloon compression, Chemical (glycerol) rhizolysis
Stereotactic Radiosurgery - involves focusing radiation on the trigeminal nerve to cause injury and prevent pain transmission, performed using Gamma Knife or X-Knife

Peripheral neurectomy – performed on branches of the trigeminal nerve (supraorbital, infraorbital, alveolar, and lingual nerves) by incision, alcohol injection, radiofrequency lesioning, or cryotherapy
